title: santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. tomentosa
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. tomentosa, also known as gray cotton lavender or woolly gray santolina, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Santolina chamaecyparissus subsp. tomentosa include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to make medicinal remedies.
